disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The child's continuous overnight tube feed became disconnected and he went roughly 6 hours without his usual feed
trigger_question_examples: What happened overnight or before this started?
Did he miss any of his feeds?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: His feeding pump came apart in the night and we didn't notice for hours — he went about six hours with no feed, which never normally happens.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: On waking he was very hard to rouse and extremely floppy/lethargic
trigger_question_examples: How was he when you tried to wake him?
Is he responding to you normally?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: We couldn't wake him properly this morning — he was completely floppy and just wouldn't come round like he normally does.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Brief episode of whole-body shaking with unresponsiveness lasting about 30 seconds
trigger_question_examples: Did he have any shaking or a fit?
Was he responsive during the shaking?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: His whole body started shaking and his eyes weren't responding for about half a minute — it was terrifying. He's never done that before.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Home glucometer reading was very low (2.1 mmol/L) around the time he woke
trigger_question_examples: Have you checked his blood sugar with his home meter?
What number did the glucometer show?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes — we checked his sugar with his home meter and it said 2.1, which is really low for him.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: He is currently awake but confused and slow to answer (altered sensorium)
trigger_question_examples: How is he answering you now?
Does he seem himself, or muddled?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He's awake now but he's really muddled — slow to answer and not making full sense.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Severe cramping in the arms and legs with visible twitching of the muscles
trigger_question_examples: Is he in any pain?
Are his muscles cramping or twitching?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: His arms and legs are cramping really badly and we can see the muscles twitching in his calves and forearms.

trigger_question_examples: What does his skin look like — colour, sweaty?
Is he clammy?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He's gone really pale and he's sweaty and clammy all over.

trigger_question_examples: How is his breathing — fast, deep, laboured?
Is he puffing or breathing hard while resting?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Now that you mention it he is breathing quite fast and a bit deep, even just lying there.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Known chronic condition requiring continuous overnight tube feeds and daytime cornstarch to keep blood sugar up
trigger_question_examples: Does he have any ongoing medical conditions?
Why does he need the overnight feeds?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He has a condition where his liver can't store and release sugar properly, so he has to be fed continuously overnight and take cornstarch during the day, or his sugar crashes.

trigger_question_examples: Has he had any fever, vomiting or diarrhoea?
Is he unwell with a tummy bug as well?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, no fever and he hasn't been vomiting or had diarrhoea — he was well yesterday, this was just the feed coming off.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No previous seizures and not on any epilepsy medication
trigger_question_examples: Has he ever had a seizure or fit before?
Is he on any epilepsy or seizure medicines?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, he's never had a fit before and he's not on any seizure medicines — this has never happened.
